title: additionalimagestores: how to update shared imagestore directory (image addition/removal)
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

After reading about the cool additionalimagestores feature at https://www.redhat.com/sysadmin/image-stores-podman I've been trying to get it working in our environment. 

I have a few questions regarding locking/concurrency around the shared imagestore directory, and thought this might be the right place to ask.   I guess you might consider this a documentation issue.

Although I have run into an issue pulling to a gluster filesystem (reported in #15302 ), overall I've been successful, in setting up and using gluster as the shared imagestore type.   :-)

Due to #15302, I ended up needing to pull to a local directory, then copy that directory to gluster.   That config leads directly to a few questions.

The basic config I have is:  as root, on gluster client box, pull to XFS volume, then (as root) cp -pr to Gluster mountpoint
   podman  --root /xfsVolume/imagestore pull someimage:someversion
   cp -pr  /xfsVolume/imagestore /mnt/glusterclient/imagestore  

Then on other boxes, I can configure /etc/containers/storage.conf and point c/storage to the gluster imagestore dir.
[storage]
additionalimagestores = [
  "/mnt/glusterclient/imagestore"
]


The questions:

Given that I need to copy a directory to the gluster (live) shared imagestore directory rather than using podman for pulling, how can I atomically update the live shared imagestore directory (on gluster) with new images, and remove old images, without breaking existing podman/cri-o usage of that shared imagestore directory?   

For example, say I have a new image IMG2 I want to push to the shared imagestore directory.   Using the gluster client box, I can pull IMG2 to my large XFS imagestore volume.   
podman  --root /xfsVolume/imagestore pull IMG2:someversion

... but I am a bit way of copying/rsyncing /xfsVolume/imagestore on top of the live gluster imagestore directory /mnt/glusterclient/imagestore , without understanding the locking mechanism.  I'm concerned I could mess up running containers using that shared imagestore.
   cp -pr  /xfsVolume/imagestore /mnt/glusterclient/imagestore      ### danger

I noticed there are files at
/var/lib/containers/storage/storage.lock
/var/lib/containers/storage/layers.lock

and I'm wondering if I should create these locks in a certain sequence, as part of this copy/update sequence.

I'm also wondering if I should perform the directory merge in a certain sequence, so that the new image appears atomically.  For example, should I merge some directory contents first?

I would appreciate any guidance or recommendations you can provide.   Thanks!



**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 4.1.1
```

comments.body:
I am able to successfully use gluster as an imagestore, so this approach it no longer necessary.  :-)

I do have one additionalimagestore question, but it's different enough from this scenario that I will open a different issue rather than repurpose this one.
